recipes.searchText: grilled ginger sesame chicken chopped salad 1/4 cup low-sodium soy sauce 2 tablespoons finely minced ginger 3 tablespoons canola oil 2 tablespoons hoisin sauce 1 tablespoon toasted sesame oil 1 teaspoon sriracha 1/2 teaspoon salt (adjust to taste) 1/4 cup red wine vinegar 1/4 cup chopped green onions 2 (9-ounce) boneless, skinless chicken breasts 1 lb napa cabbage, halved lengthwise and thinly sliced crosswise 1 1/2 to 2 cups matchstick carrots 2/3 cup slivered or sliced almonds, toasted 1/2 cup cilantro leaves, chopped 3 green onions, chopped 1 teaspoon white sesame seeds, toasted 1 teaspoon black sesame seeds whisk together soy sauce, ginger, canola oil, hoisin sauce, sesame oil, sriracha, and salt in a mixing bowl. add chicken breasts to a resealable bag with 3 tablespoons of the marinade mixture, reserving the remainder. seal and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 1 day. combine red wine vinegar and 1/4 cup chopped green onions with the reserved marinade mixture to make the dressing. set aside. heat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat. brush lightly with oil and cook marinated chicken about 4 minutes per side until cooked through (165 degrees f internal). transfer chicken to a cutting board, rest 10 minutes, then cut crosswise into 1/4-inch thick strips. in a large bowl, toss cabbage, chicken, carrots, almonds, chopped green onions, and cilantro with enough dressing to coat. sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve.

recipes.title: Green Bean Caprese Salad
ingredients: 8-12 small mozzarella balls, drained and halved
Pinch of sea salt and freshly cracked black pepper
instructions: Steam green beans for 5-7 minutes, until tender but still bright green. Rinse in cold water to stop cooking. Set aside.
Prepare the balsamic vinaigrette by combining garlic, vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper in a jar. Shake well or whisk until combined.
Slice tomato into 1/4-inch pieces and arrange around the outer edge of a plate. Halve mozzarella balls and sprinkle over tomato slices.
Sprinkle basil over the tomato and mozzarella.
Arrange steamed green beans in the center of the plate on top of the tomatoes.
Pour vinaigrette over everything. Let sit for a few minutes to absorb flavors.
Top with remaining vinaigrette and any leftover mozzarella and basil before serving.
recipes.searchText: green bean caprese salad 3/4 pound fresh green beans, trimmed 1 large (or 2 medium) heirloom tomatoes, sliced 8-12 small mozzarella balls, drained and halved 1/4 cup fresh basil, julienned 1/2 clove garlic, minced 1 1/2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar 2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil pinch of sea salt and freshly cracked black pepper steam green beans for 5-7 minutes, until tender but still bright green. rinse in cold water to stop cooking. set aside. prepare the balsamic vinaigrette by combining garlic, vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper in a jar. shake well or whisk until combined. slice tomato into 1/4-inch pieces and arrange around the outer edge of a plate. halve mozzarella balls and sprinkle over tomato slices. sprinkle basil over the tomato and mozzarella. arrange steamed green beans in the center of the plate on top of the tomatoes. pour vinaigrette over everything. let sit for a few minutes to absorb flavors. top with remaining vinaigrette and any leftover mozzarella and basil before serving.

recipes.title: Grilled Chicken Skewers with Peanut Sauce

items: 1 1/2 pounds boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into 12 strips

instructions: Combine soy sauce, sesame oil, honey, vinegar, garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes. Submerge chicken strips in marinade, refrigerate for at least 5 hours or overnight.
Blend all peanut sauce ingredients until smooth, adjusting water as needed. Refrigerate until serving.
Thread marinated chicken onto skewers and chill. Heat remaining marinade in saucepan until thickened.
Oil grill grates and heat to medium-high. Cook skewers 3-4 minutes per side until cooked through, basting frequently.
Serve with peanut sauce.
recipes.searchText: grilled chicken skewers with peanut sauce 1 1/2 pounds boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into 12 strips 1/2 cup lite soy sauce (or tamari) 2 tablespoons sesame oil 2 tablespoons honey 1 teaspoon balsamic vinegar 2 garlic cloves, minced 2 teaspoons fresh grated ginger 2 teaspoons red pepper flakes 1/2 cup natural peanut butter 1 tablespoon honey 2 tablespoons lite soy sauce (or tamari) 2 teaspoons fresh grated ginger 2 tablespoons fresh lime juice 1 garlic clove, minced 1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes 1/4 cup water combine soy sauce, sesame oil, honey, vinegar, garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes. submerge chicken strips in marinade, refrigerate for at least 5 hours or overnight. blend all peanut sauce ingredients until smooth, adjusting water as needed. refrigerate until serving. thread marinated chicken onto skewers and chill. heat remaining marinade in saucepan until thickened. oil grill grates and heat to medium-high. cook skewers 3-4 minutes per side until cooked through, basting frequently. serve with peanut sauce.

recipes.title: Grown-Up Mac and Cheese
ingredients: 3 ounces extra sharp cheddar cheese, grated
2 slices white sandwich bread, crusts removed
instructions: Preheat oven to 400 degrees F. Arrange bacon on a baking rack set on a sheet pan. Bake 15-20 minutes until crispy. Drain on paper towels and crumble when cooled.
Boil salted water with oil in a large pot. Cook macaroni 6-8 minutes and drain well.
Warm milk in a small saucepan without boiling.
Melt butter in a medium pan and add flour. Cook 2 minutes while whisking constantly.
Gradually add hot milk while whisking. Cook until thickened.
Stir in gruyere, cheddar, blue cheese, salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
Fold in cooked macaroni and crumbled bacon.
Divide into 2 gratin dishes.
Pulse bread in a food processor to coarse crumbs, add basil and pulse again. Top pasta with breadcrumb mixture.
Bake 35-40 minutes until golden and bubbly.
recipes.searchText: grown-up mac and cheese 1/4 pound thick-sliced bacon 1 teaspoon vegetable oil kosher salt 2 cups elbow macaroni or cavatappi 1 1/2 cups milk 2 tablespoons unsalted butter 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour 4 ounces gruyere cheese, grated 3 ounces extra sharp cheddar cheese, grated 2 ounces blue cheese, crumbled 1/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper pinch of ground nutmeg 2 slices white sandwich bread, crusts removed 2 tablespoons fresh basil leaves, chopped preheat oven to 400 degrees f. arrange bacon on a baking rack set on a sheet pan. bake 15-20 minutes until crispy. drain on paper towels and crumble when cooled. boil salted water with oil in a large pot. cook macaroni 6-8 minutes and drain well. warm milk in a small saucepan without boiling. melt butter in a medium pan and add flour. cook 2 minutes while whisking constantly. gradually add hot milk while whisking. cook until thickened. stir in gruyere, cheddar, blue cheese, salt, pepper, and nutmeg. fold in cooked macaroni and crumbled bacon. divide into 2 gratin dishes. pulse bread in a food processor to coarse crumbs, add basil and pulse again. top pasta with breadcrumb mixture. bake 35-40 minutes until golden and bubbly.

recipes.title: Ham and Potato Soup
ingredients: Ground white or black pepper to taste
instructions: Combine potatoes, celery, onion, ham, and chicken broth in a stockpot. Bring to a boil, then cook over medium heat until potatoes are tender, about 10-15 minutes. Stir in salt and pepper.

In a separate saucepan, melt butter over medium-low heat. Whisk in flour with a fork and cook, stirring constantly until thick, about 1 minute. Slowly stir in milk so that lumps don't form until all of the milk has been added. Continue stirring over medium-low heat until thick, 3-4 minutes.
Stir the milk mixture into the stockpot and cook soup until heated through. Add more milk or chicken broth to thin if needed.
Serve topped with cheddar cheese, chives, and bacon if desired.
recipes.searchText: ham and potato soup 3 1/2 cups peeled and diced potatoes 1/3 cup diced celery 1/3 cup finely chopped onion 1 cup diced cooked ham 3 1/4 cups low-sodium chicken broth 1/2 teaspoon salt, or to taste ground white or black pepper to taste 5 tablespoons butter 5 tablespoons all-purpose flour 2 cups whole milk combine potatoes, celery, onion, ham, and chicken broth in a stockpot. bring to a boil, then cook over medium heat until potatoes are tender, about 10-15 minutes. stir in salt and pepper. in a separate saucepan, melt butter over medium-low heat. whisk in flour with a fork and cook, stirring constantly until thick, about 1 minute. slowly stir in milk so that lumps don't form until all of the milk has been added. continue stirring over medium-low heat until thick, 3-4 minutes. stir the milk mixture into the stockpot and cook soup until heated through. add more milk or chicken broth to thin if needed. serve topped with cheddar cheese, chives, and bacon if desired.

recipes.title: How to Cook Steak in the Oven
ingredients: 2 steaks, about 1 to 1.5 inches thick (filet mignon, rib eye, T-bone, strip, or sirloin)
1 tablespoon avocado oil or other high-heat cooking oil
instructions: Let steaks rest on a plate until they reach room temperature, about 30-45 minutes.
Preheat oven broiler on high. Place an oven-safe skillet (cast iron recommended) in the oven while it preheats.
In a small bowl, whisk together salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Blot steaks dry with paper towels. Brush evenly on both sides with oil, then sprinkle seasoning mixture on both sides.
Carefully remove the hot skillet from the oven and place on the stovetop over high heat. Sear steaks for 2-3 minutes on one side until a crust forms.
Flip the steaks, then transfer the skillet to the oven. Broil for 3 minutes.
Flip steaks once more and continue broiling for 2-3 minutes, until internal temperature reaches 5 degrees below your desired doneness.
Remove pan from oven and transfer steaks to a clean plate. Brush with pan juices, top with butter and thyme. Loosely tent with foil and rest for at least 3 minutes.
recipes.notes:
## Temperature Guide
- Rare: 120-125 degrees F
- Medium-Rare: 130-135 degrees F
- Medium: 135-145 degrees F
- Medium-Well: 145-155 degrees F
- Well Done: 155+ degrees F
recipes.searchText: how to cook steak in the oven 2 steaks, about 1 to 1.5 inches thick (filet mignon, rib eye, t-bone, strip, or sirloin) 1 teaspoon salt 1/2 teaspoon black pepper 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder 1 tablespoon avocado oil or other high-heat cooking oil 2 tablespoons butter (for serving) fresh thyme sprigs (for serving) let steaks rest on a plate until they reach room temperature, about 30-45 minutes. preheat oven broiler on high. place an oven-safe skillet (cast iron recommended) in the oven while it preheats. in a small bowl, whisk together salt, pepper, and garlic powder. blot steaks dry with paper towels. brush evenly on both sides with oil, then sprinkle seasoning mixture on both sides. carefully remove the hot skillet from the oven and place on the stovetop over high heat. sear steaks for 2-3 minutes on one side until a crust forms. flip the steaks, then transfer the skillet to the oven. broil for 3 minutes. flip steaks once more and continue broiling for 2-3 minutes, until internal temperature reaches 5 degrees below your desired doneness. remove pan from oven and transfer steaks to a clean plate. brush with pan juices, top with butter and thyme. loosely tent with foil and rest for at least 3 minutes.

recipes.title: Italian Wedding Soup

items: 1/2 lb sweet or hot Italian sausage, removed from casings

instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Line a baking sheet with foil and place an oven-safe rack on top. Spray generously with cooking spray.
Whisk together egg, chives, sage, and garlic in a large bowl.
Add remaining meatball ingredients and mix gently by hand until combined.
Form into tablespoon-sized balls and arrange on the prepared rack.
Bake meatballs 15-18 minutes until lightly browned and cooked through.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook 8 minutes, stirring frequently, until softened.
Add broths, water, wine, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil.
Add pasta and cook 8-10 minutes until al dente, stirring occasionally.
Reduce heat to low. Add spinach and meatballs. Simmer a few minutes until spinach wilts and meatballs heat through.
Ladle into bowls and serve with grated Parmigiano Reggiano.
recipes.searchText: italian wedding soup 1 large egg 3 tablespoons finely chopped fresh chives 2 teaspoons finely chopped fresh sage 2 cloves garlic, minced 3/4 lb 85% or 90% lean ground beef 1/2 lb sweet or hot italian sausage, removed from casings 1/2 cup grated parmigiano reggiano 1/3 cup italian seasoned bread crumbs 1/4 teaspoon salt 2 tablespoons olive oil 1 medium yellow onion, diced 2 large carrots, diced 2 stalks celery, diced 6 cups chicken broth 2 cups beef broth 2 cups water 1/2 cup dry white wine (optional) 1 bay leaf 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/4 teaspoon white pepper 1 cup small pasta (dittalini or similar) 4 ounces fresh spinach, roughly chopped parmigiano reggiano, for serving preheat oven to 350 degrees f. line a baking sheet with foil and place an oven-safe rack on top. spray generously with cooking spray. whisk together egg, chives, sage, and garlic in a large bowl. add remaining meatball ingredients and mix gently by hand until combined. form into tablespoon-sized balls and arrange on the prepared rack. bake meatballs 15-18 minutes until lightly browned and cooked through. heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. add onion, carrots, and celery. cook 8 minutes, stirring frequently, until softened. add broths, water, wine, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. bring to a boil. add pasta and cook 8-10 minutes until al dente, stirring occasionally. reduce heat to low. add spinach and meatballs. simmer a few minutes until spinach wilts and meatballs heat through. ladle into bowls and serve with grated parmigiano reggiano.

recipes.title: Lemon Orzo Salad
ingredients: 3 tablespoons lemon juice (fresh squeezed, about 1 medium lemon)
instructions: Fill a 5 qt saucepan halfway with water and add a large pinch of salt. Bring to a boil over high heat. Add the orzo and return to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook the pasta for about 2 minutes less than the box indicates. You want the orzo to be just cooked but still firm. While the pasta is cooking, add the frozen peas to the bottom of the colander you will use to drain the orzo.
When the pasta is cooked, drain it over the frozen peas. This will defrost and cook the peas just slightly. Rinse the pasta and peas under cold water to stop the cooking process, then set aside.
In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the lemon zest, lemon juice, olive oil, chopped dill, and salt. Add the pasta and peas and fold until completely combined.

Stir in crumbled feta to combine, then cover the bowl with plastic wrap and chill for at least 1 hour. Before serving, give the salad one last stir to redistribute the dressing and break up any pasta that stuck together. Taste for seasoning and add salt and pepper if needed. Serve cold or at room temperature.
recipes.searchText: lemon orzo salad 16 oz box orzo pasta 1 cup frozen peas 1/2 lemon, zested 3 tablespoons lemon juice (fresh squeezed, about 1 medium lemon) 4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil 1-2 tablespoons dill, chopped and packed 1/2 teaspoon salt 6 oz greek feta cheese, crumbled salt and pepper, to taste fill a 5 qt saucepan halfway with water and add a large pinch of salt. bring to a boil over high heat. add the orzo and return to a boil. reduce heat to medium-low and cook the pasta for about 2 minutes less than the box indicates. you want the orzo to be just cooked but still firm. while the pasta is cooking, add the frozen peas to the bottom of the colander you will use to drain the orzo. when the pasta is cooked, drain it over the frozen peas. this will defrost and cook the peas just slightly. rinse the pasta and peas under cold water to stop the cooking process, then set aside. in a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the lemon zest, lemon juice, olive oil, chopped dill, and salt. add the pasta and peas and fold until completely combined. stir in crumbled feta to combine, then cover the bowl with plastic wrap and chill for at least 1 hour. before serving, give the salad one last stir to redistribute the dressing and break up any pasta that stuck together. taste for seasoning and add salt and pepper if needed. serve cold or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Lemon Ricotta Pasta with Spinach
ingredients: 1/2 lb (8 oz) pasta (spaghetti, linguine, penne, or fusilli)
1/3 cup grated Parmesan cheese, plus extra to serve
1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil, plus extra for drizzling
Salt and black pepper, to taste
instructions: In a large pot of boiling salted water, cook pasta according to package directions until al dente. Meanwhile, make the ricotta sauce.

In a medium bowl, combine ricotta, olive oil, Parmesan cheese, garlic, lemon zest, and juice. Season with 1/4 teaspoon of salt and a good pinch of pepper. Stir until well combined and taste for seasoning.
In the last minute of the pasta's cooking time, reserve 1/2 cup of the cooking water, then add spinach to the pot. Stir well and push the leaves down to submerge them in water.
After 1 minute, drain and return pasta and spinach to the same pot.
Add the ricotta sauce and part of the reserved cooking water. Stir well to evenly coat the pasta in the sauce, adding more cooking water as needed for a smooth and creamy texture.
Serve immediately and garnish with grated or shaved Parmesan cheese, a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil, and lemon wedges if desired. Add red pepper flakes if you like.
recipes.searchText: lemon ricotta pasta with spinach 1/2 lb (8 oz) pasta (spaghetti, linguine, penne, or fusilli) 1 cup whole-milk ricotta 8 oz fresh baby spinach, washed 1/3 cup grated parmesan cheese, plus extra to serve 1 unwaxed lemon, zest and juice 3 lemon wedges, to serve (optional) 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil, plus extra for drizzling 1 garlic clove, grated or pressed salt and black pepper, to taste in a large pot of boiling salted water, cook pasta according to package directions until al dente. meanwhile, make the ricotta sauce. in a medium bowl, combine ricotta, olive oil, parmesan cheese, garlic, lemon zest, and juice. season with 1/4 teaspoon of salt and a good pinch of pepper. stir until well combined and taste for seasoning. in the last minute of the pasta's cooking time, reserve 1/2 cup of the cooking water, then add spinach to the pot. stir well and push the leaves down to submerge them in water. after 1 minute, drain and return pasta and spinach to the same pot. add the ricotta sauce and part of the reserved cooking water. stir well to evenly coat the pasta in the sauce, adding more cooking water as needed for a smooth and creamy texture. serve immediately and garnish with grated or shaved parmesan cheese, a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil, and lemon wedges if desired. add red pepper flakes if you like.

recipes.title: Lemon Spaghetti
instructions: Cook the pasta in salted water per package directions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, whisk together olive oil, Parmesan, 1 tablespoon of the lemon zest, and lemon juice in a large serving bowl.
Drain pasta and transfer to the serving bowl with lemon sauce. Toss to coat.
Add pepper and salt, then toss. Adjust seasoning to taste.
Add chopped parsley and toss. Top with remaining lemon zest.
Serve warm.
recipes.searchText: lemon spaghetti 1 pound thin spaghetti 1/2 cup olive oil 2/3 cup parmesan cheese, shredded 1/2 cup fresh lemon juice (2-3 lemons) 2 tablespoons lemon zest, divided 1/4 cup fresh italian parsley, chopped 1 teaspoon black pepper 1/2 teaspoon salt cook the pasta in salted water per package directions until al dente. while pasta cooks, whisk together olive oil, parmesan, 1 tablespoon of the lemon zest, and lemon juice in a large serving bowl. drain pasta and transfer to the serving bowl with lemon sauce. toss to coat. add pepper and salt, then toss. adjust seasoning to taste. add chopped parsley and toss. top with remaining lemon zest. serve warm.

recipes.title: Lemony Chicken Stew with Orzo
ingredients: 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
3 tablespoons lemon juice (or more to taste)
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons oil in an 8-quart stockpot over medium-high heat. Season flour with salt and pepper, then dredge chicken pieces in the flour mixture.
Brown half the chicken until golden, about 2 minutes per side, then transfer to a bowl. Add 2 more tablespoons oil and brown remaining chicken. Set aside with the first batch.

Add remaining 1 tablespoon oil to pot with carrots, celery, and onion. Cook over medium heat for 3-5 minutes until onion softens. Add leek and garlic and cook 2 more minutes. Season with remaining salt and pepper.
Return chicken to pot with orzo, chicken stock, water, and half the tarragon. Cover and simmer about 15 minutes until orzo is tender and broth thickens slightly, stirring occasionally.
Stir in lemon juice and season with additional salt and pepper as needed. Sprinkle with remaining tarragon before serving.
recipes.searchText: lemony chicken stew with orzo 5 tablespoons canola oil, divided 1/4 cup all-purpose flour 1 teaspoon kosher salt, divided 1 teaspoon freshly ground pepper, divided 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1/2-inch pieces 4 carrots, peeled and sliced 2 ribs celery, chopped 1 onion, diced 1 leek, thinly sliced 2 cloves garlic, minced 1 cup orzo pasta 5 cups chicken stock 1 cup water 3 tablespoons lemon juice (or more to taste) 2 tablespoons fresh tarragon, chopped heat 2 tablespoons oil in an 8-quart stockpot over medium-high heat. season flour with salt and pepper, then dredge chicken pieces in the flour mixture. brown half the chicken until golden, about 2 minutes per side, then transfer to a bowl. add 2 more tablespoons oil and brown remaining chicken. set aside with the first batch. add remaining 1 tablespoon oil to pot with carrots, celery, and onion. cook over medium heat for 3-5 minutes until onion softens. add leek and garlic and cook 2 more minutes. season with remaining salt and pepper. return chicken to pot with orzo, chicken stock, water, and half the tarragon. cover and simmer about 15 minutes until orzo is tender and broth thickens slightly, stirring occasionally. stir in lemon juice and season with additional salt and pepper as needed. sprinkle with remaining tarragon before serving.

recipes.title: Impossibly Easy Mini Chicken and Broccoli Pies
ingredients: 1 lb boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into bite-size pieces
1 cup frozen chopped broccoli, thawed and drained
instructions: Heat oven to 375 degrees F. Spray 12 regular-size muffin cups with cooking spray.

In a 10-inch nonstick skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until no longer pink in center. Add onion and cook 2-3 minutes. Add broccoli, salt, and pepper, stirring occasionally until heated through. Cool 5 minutes, then stir in cheese.

In a medium bowl, whisk together Bisquick mix, milk, and eggs until blended. Spoon 1 scant tablespoon baking mixture into each muffin cup. Top with about 1/4 cup chicken-broccoli mixture. Spoon 1 tablespoon baking mixture onto chicken mixture in each cup.

Bake about 30 minutes until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean and tops are golden brown. Cool 5 minutes. Loosen sides with a thin knife, remove from pan, and cool 10 minutes longer before serving.
recipes.searchText: impossibly easy mini chicken and broccoli pies 1 tablespoon vegetable oil 1 lb boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into bite-size pieces 1 medium onion, chopped (1/2 cup) 1 cup frozen chopped broccoli, thawed and drained 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/4 teaspoon pepper 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese (4 oz) 1/2 cup original bisquick mix 1/2 cup milk 2 eggs heat oven to 375 degrees f. spray 12 regular-size muffin cups with cooking spray. in a 10-inch nonstick skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. cook chicken 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until no longer pink in center. add onion and cook 2-3 minutes. add broccoli, salt, and pepper, stirring occasionally until heated through. cool 5 minutes, then stir in cheese. in a medium bowl, whisk together bisquick mix, milk, and eggs until blended. spoon 1 scant tablespoon baking mixture into each muffin cup. top with about 1/4 cup chicken-broccoli mixture. spoon 1 tablespoon baking mixture onto chicken mixture in each cup. bake about 30 minutes until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean and tops are golden brown. cool 5 minutes. loosen sides with a thin knife, remove from pan, and cool 10 minutes longer before serving.

recipes.title: Mojo Roast Chicken (Pollo Asado)
ingredients: 1 (6 lb) whole chicken, cut into pieces
instructions: Heat olive oil in a small saucepan over medium-high heat until very hot. Mash cumin, salt, oregano, and garlic into a paste in a heat-proof bowl; whisk hot oil into the paste. Allow to cool slightly. Add lime juice and orange juice; stir.
Place chicken in a large sealable bag; pour marinade over chicken and shake for even coating. Refrigerate at least 4 hours, up to overnight.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees F. Arrange marinated chicken in a roasting pan.
Bake until no longer pink at the bone and juices run clear, about 1 hour. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the thigh near the bone should read 180 degrees F.
recipes.searchText: mojo roast chicken (pollo asado) 1/2 cup extra-virgin olive oil 1 1/2 teaspoons ground cumin 1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt 1/2 teaspoon dried oregano 2 tablespoons minced garlic 3 tablespoons lime juice 3 tablespoons orange juice 1 (6 lb) whole chicken, cut into pieces heat olive oil in a small saucepan over medium-high heat until very hot. mash cumin, salt, oregano, and garlic into a paste in a heat-proof bowl; whisk hot oil into the paste. allow to cool slightly. add lime juice and orange juice; stir. place chicken in a large sealable bag; pour marinade over chicken and shake for even coating. refrigerate at least 4 hours, up to overnight. preheat oven to 375 degrees f. arrange marinated chicken in a roasting pan. bake until no longer pink at the bone and juices run clear, about 1 hour. an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the thigh near the bone should read 180 degrees f.

recipes.title: Indian Mulligatawny Soup
ingredients: 1/4 cup butter (or vegetable oil for vegan)
2 teaspoons peeled and minced ginger root
2 small firm apples, peeled, cored and diced
Salt and black pepper to taste
Chopped cilantro and/or scallions for garnish
instructions: Melt the butter in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the onion, carrot, and jalapeno, then saute for 4 to 5 minutes or until the onions have softened.
Add the garlic, ginger, apples, and diced tomatoes to the pot. Saute for another 3 minutes. Then add in all of the spices and toss to coat.
Add in the lentils and broth and let the contents come to a boil. Turn the heat down to medium-low and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes.
Puree about 75% of the ingredients using either an immersion blender or by transferring a portion of the contents to the bowl of a standard blender. Leave some of the chunks whole for texture.
Stir in the coconut milk, season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve garnished with roasted cashews and chopped cilantro or scallions.
recipes.searchText: indian mulligatawny soup 1/4 cup butter (or vegetable oil for vegan) 1 yellow onion, chopped 1 carrot, peeled and diced 1 red jalapeno, seeded and diced 3 garlic cloves, minced 2 teaspoons peeled and minced ginger root 2 small firm apples, peeled, cored and diced 1 (14.5 oz) can diced tomatoes 1 tablespoon curry powder 1 teaspoon ground cumin 1/2 teaspoon paprika 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon 1/2 teaspoon ground turmeric 1/4 teaspoon ground cardamom 1/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper 1/2 teaspoon dried thyme 1/2 cup red lentils, uncooked 3 cups chicken or vegetable broth 2/3 cup canned unsweetened coconut milk salt and black pepper to taste roasted cashews for garnish chopped cilantro and/or scallions for garnish melt the butter in a large pot or dutch oven over medium-high heat. add the onion, carrot, and jalapeno, then saute for 4 to 5 minutes or until the onions have softened. add the garlic, ginger, apples, and diced tomatoes to the pot. saute for another 3 minutes. then add in all of the spices and toss to coat. add in the lentils and broth and let the contents come to a boil. turn the heat down to medium-low and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes. puree about 75% of the ingredients using either an immersion blender or by transferring a portion of the contents to the bowl of a standard blender. leave some of the chunks whole for texture. stir in the coconut milk, season with salt and pepper to taste. serve garnished with roasted cashews and chopped cilantro or scallions.

recipes.title: No-Bake Energy Bites
instructions: Stir all ingredients together in a medium bowl until thoroughly mixed.
Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes to 1 hour so the mixture is easier to handle.
Roll into balls about 1 inch in diameter.
Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
recipes.searchText: no-bake energy bites 1 cup old-fashioned oats 2/3 cup toasted shredded coconut 1/2 cup creamy peanut butter 1/2 cup ground flaxseed 1/2 cup semisweet chocolate chips 1/3 cup honey 1 tablespoon chia seeds (optional) 1 teaspoon vanilla extract stir all ingredients together in a medium bowl until thoroughly mixed. cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes to 1 hour so the mixture is easier to handle. roll into balls about 1 inch in diameter. store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

recipes.title: No Peek Chicken
ingredients: 1 (10.5 oz) can cream of chicken soup
1 (10.5 oz) can cream of mushroom soup
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly spray a 9x13 inch baking dish with cooking spray.
Pour cream of chicken soup, cream of mushroom soup, chicken broth, rice, and onion soup mix into the baking dish. Whisk together until combined.
Place chicken breasts on top of the rice mixture.
Cover tightly with aluminum foil.
Bake for 1 hour 15 minutes to 1 hour 30 minutes. Do not peek or remove the foil during cooking -- the steam is what cooks the rice and keeps the chicken tender.
recipes.searchText: no peek chicken 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts 1 (10.5 oz) can cream of chicken soup 1 (10.5 oz) can cream of mushroom soup 2 cups chicken broth 2 cups uncooked long-grain white rice 1 packet onion soup mix preheat oven to 350°f. lightly spray a 9x13 inch baking dish with cooking spray. pour cream of chicken soup, cream of mushroom soup, chicken broth, rice, and onion soup mix into the baking dish. whisk together until combined. place chicken breasts on top of the rice mixture. cover tightly with aluminum foil. bake for 1 hour 15 minutes to 1 hour 30 minutes. do not peek or remove the foil during cooking -- the steam is what cooks the rice and keeps the chicken tender.

recipes.title: Oatmeal Flax Blueberry Muffins

items: 1/2 cup rolled oats or old-fashioned oatmeal
1/2 cup buttermilk (or regular milk plus 1 teaspoon vinegar)
1 tablespoon cold butter, cut into small pieces

instructions: Heat oven to 375 degrees F. Grease a muffin tin with butter and dust with flour, or use paper liners.
Combine oats and buttermilk in a mixing bowl. Allow to soak for 15-20 minutes. Add melted butter, egg, brown sugar, and vanilla. Stir by hand until just combined.
Add flour, flaxmeal, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Stir until evenly moistened.
Scoop a spoonful of batter into each muffin cup first. Gently fold blueberries into remaining batter and distribute evenly. Top each with 2-3 additional blueberries.
For the topping, press cold butter, flour, and brown sugar together with your fingers until fine crumbs form. Sprinkle over each muffin before baking.
Bake 13-15 minutes until golden brown. While cooling, mix powdered sugar and milk for glaze. Drizzle over cooled muffins before serving.
recipes.searchText: oatmeal flax blueberry muffins 1/2 cup rolled oats or old-fashioned oatmeal 1/2 cup buttermilk (or regular milk plus 1 teaspoon vinegar) 1 1/2 tablespoons salted butter, melted 1 egg 1/3 cup brown sugar 1/2 teaspoon vanilla 1/2 cup plus 1 tablespoon flour 1 heaping tablespoon ground flaxmeal 1/2 teaspoon baking powder 1/2 teaspoon baking soda 1/4 teaspoon salt 1/2 cup fresh blueberries 1 tablespoon cold butter, cut into small pieces 3 tablespoons flour 1 tablespoon brown sugar 1/4 cup powdered sugar 1/2 tablespoon milk heat oven to 375 degrees f. grease a muffin tin with butter and dust with flour, or use paper liners. combine oats and buttermilk in a mixing bowl. allow to soak for 15-20 minutes. add melted butter, egg, brown sugar, and vanilla. stir by hand until just combined. add flour, flaxmeal, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. stir until evenly moistened. scoop a spoonful of batter into each muffin cup first. gently fold blueberries into remaining batter and distribute evenly. top each with 2-3 additional blueberries. for the topping, press cold butter, flour, and brown sugar together with your fingers until fine crumbs form. sprinkle over each muffin before baking. bake 13-15 minutes until golden brown. while cooling, mix powdered sugar and milk for glaze. drizzle over cooled muffins before serving.

recipes.title: Elegant Orzo with Wilted Spinach and Pine Nuts
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add orzo and cook for 8 to 10 minutes. Drain and transfer to a mixing bowl.
Heat olive oil and butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Stir in garlic, basil, and red pepper flakes. Reduce heat to medium.
Stir in pine nuts and cook until lightly browned.
Add spinach, cover, and cook on low heat for 5 minutes or until spinach is wilted.
Combine spinach mixture with cooked orzo pasta.
Plate and drizzle with balsamic vinegar. Top with feta cheese and chopped tomatoes. Season with salt to taste.
recipes.searchText: elegant orzo with wilted spinach and pine nuts 1 (16 oz) package uncooked orzo pasta 1/2 cup olive oil 2 tablespoons butter 1/2 teaspoon minced garlic 1/2 teaspoon dried basil 1/2 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes 1 cup pine nuts 1 (10 oz) bag baby spinach 1/8 cup balsamic vinegar 1 (8 oz) package crumbled feta cheese 1/2 fresh tomato, chopped salt to taste bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. add orzo and cook for 8 to 10 minutes. drain and transfer to a mixing bowl. heat olive oil and butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. stir in garlic, basil, and red pepper flakes. reduce heat to medium. stir in pine nuts and cook until lightly browned. add spinach, cover, and cook on low heat for 5 minutes or until spinach is wilted. combine spinach mixture with cooked orzo pasta. plate and drizzle with balsamic vinegar. top with feta cheese and chopped tomatoes. season with salt to taste.

recipes.title: Simple Oven Baked Brisket
ingredients: 4 lb beef brisket, trimmed with a thin layer of fat remaining
1/2 cup prepared horseradish sour cream sauce (optional, for serving)
instructions: Preheat oven to 325 degrees F. Prepare a roasting pan with a rack.
Combine salt, pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, and paprika in a small bowl. Rub this seasoning mixture on all sides of the brisket.
Place brisket fat-side up on the roasting rack in the pan. Add enough beef broth to cover the bottom of the pan by approximately half an inch.
Cover tightly with aluminum foil (use two pieces for a secure seal) or a lid. Bake for 1 hour.
Lower temperature to 275 degrees F and continue cooking, covered, for an additional 3 hours or until fork-tender. Monitor liquid level periodically and add more broth if needed.
Cool approximately 15 minutes while covered, then slice thinly. Trim any excess fat from the top if desired.
Serve with horseradish sour cream and/or sharp mustard. Drizzle or soak slices in the leftover pan sauce to maintain moisture.
recipes.searchText: simple oven baked brisket 4 lb beef brisket, trimmed with a thin layer of fat remaining 1 tablespoon salt 1/2 teaspoon black pepper 1 teaspoon garlic powder 1 teaspoon onion powder 2 tablespoons paprika 2 1/2 cups beef broth 1/4 cup sharp mustard (optional, for serving) 1/2 cup prepared horseradish sour cream sauce (optional, for serving) preheat oven to 325 degrees f. prepare a roasting pan with a rack. combine salt, pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, and paprika in a small bowl. rub this seasoning mixture on all sides of the brisket. place brisket fat-side up on the roasting rack in the pan. add enough beef broth to cover the bottom of the pan by approximately half an inch. cover tightly with aluminum foil (use two pieces for a secure seal) or a lid. bake for 1 hour. lower temperature to 275 degrees f and continue cooking, covered, for an additional 3 hours or until fork-tender. monitor liquid level periodically and add more broth if needed. cool approximately 15 minutes while covered, then slice thinly. trim any excess fat from the top if desired. serve with horseradish sour cream and/or sharp mustard. drizzle or soak slices in the leftover pan sauce to maintain moisture.

recipes.title: One Pan Crispy Parmesan Garlic Chicken with Vegetables
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
In a shallow dish, combine panko breadcrumbs, Parmesan cheese, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Dip each chicken breast in melted butter, then coat in the breadcrumb mixture. Place in a 9x13 baking dish.
Toss zucchini, yellow squash, and potatoes with olive oil and Italian seasoning. Arrange vegetables around the chicken in the pan.
Bake uncovered for 30-35 minutes, then cover with foil and bake an additional 10-15 minutes until chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165 degrees F and vegetables are tender.
recipes.searchText: one pan crispy parmesan garlic chicken with vegetables 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts 1 cup panko breadcrumbs 1/2 cup freshly grated parmesan cheese 1 teaspoon garlic powder 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/4 teaspoon pepper 1/2 cup butter, melted 1 medium zucchini, sliced 1 medium yellow squash, sliced 1/2 pound baby red potatoes, quartered 2 tablespoons olive oil 1 teaspoon italian seasoning preheat oven to 350 degrees f. in a shallow dish, combine panko breadcrumbs, parmesan cheese, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. dip each chicken breast in melted butter, then coat in the breadcrumb mixture. place in a 9x13 baking dish. toss zucchini, yellow squash, and potatoes with olive oil and italian seasoning. arrange vegetables around the chicken in the pan. bake uncovered for 30-35 minutes, then cover with foil and bake an additional 10-15 minutes until chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165 degrees f and vegetables are tender.

recipes.title: Copycat Olive Garden Pasta E Fagioli Soup
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 (15 oz) can dark red kidney beans, drained and rinsed
1 (15 oz) can great northern beans, drained and rinsed
Finely shredded Romano or Parmesan cheese for serving
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown the ground beef, stirring occasionally until fully cooked. Drain excess fat and transfer beef to a plate.
Heat remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil in the same pot. Add onions, carrots, and celery and saute over medium-high heat for about 6 minutes until tender. Add garlic and cook 1 minute longer.
Add chicken broth, tomato sauce, water, canned tomatoes, sugar, basil, oregano, thyme, marjoram, and cooked beef. Season with salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low. Cover and simmer 15 to 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until vegetables soften.
Meanwhile, prepare ditalini pasta according to package directions to al dente. Drain.
Add cooked pasta to soup along with kidney beans and great northern beans. Thin with additional broth or water if desired.
Cook 1 minute longer. Stir in parsley and serve warm with grated Romano or Parmesan cheese.
recipes.searchText: copycat olive garden pasta e fagioli soup 2 tablespoons olive oil, divided 1 lb lean ground beef 1 1/2 cups chopped yellow onion 1 cup diced carrots (about 2 medium) 1 cup diced celery (about 3 stalks) 3 cloves garlic, minced (1 tablespoon) 3 (8 oz) cans tomato sauce 2 (14.5 oz) cans low-sodium chicken broth 1/2 cup water (plus more as desired) 1 (15 oz) can diced tomatoes 2 teaspoons granulated sugar 1 1/2 teaspoons dried basil 1 teaspoon dried oregano 3/4 teaspoon dried thyme 1/2 teaspoon dried marjoram salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste 1 cup dry ditalini pasta 1 (15 oz) can dark red kidney beans, drained and rinsed 1 (15 oz) can great northern beans, drained and rinsed 3 tablespoons minced fresh parsley finely shredded romano or parmesan cheese for serving heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. brown the ground beef, stirring occasionally until fully cooked. drain excess fat and transfer beef to a plate. heat remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil in the same pot. add onions, carrots, and celery and saute over medium-high heat for about 6 minutes until tender. add garlic and cook 1 minute longer. add chicken broth, tomato sauce, water, canned tomatoes, sugar, basil, oregano, thyme, marjoram, and cooked beef. season with salt and pepper. bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low. cover and simmer 15 to 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until vegetables soften. meanwhile, prepare ditalini pasta according to package directions to al dente. drain. add cooked pasta to soup along with kidney beans and great northern beans. thin with additional broth or water if desired. cook 1 minute longer. stir in parsley and serve warm with grated romano or parmesan cheese.

recipes.title: Philly Cheese Potatoes
ingredients: 3 lb Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and cut into small chunks
1 can (14.5 oz) fat-free reduced-sodium chicken broth
1 tub (8 oz) Philadelphia Cream Cheese Spread
instructions: Cook potatoes in boiling water in a large saucepan for 15 minutes or until tender. Drain.
Mix broth and seasonings in a microwaveable bowl until blended. Microwave on high for 1 1/2 minutes or until heated through.
Return potatoes to the saucepan. Add broth mixture.
Mash potatoes to desired consistency.
Add cream cheese and stir until completely melted and mashed potatoes are well blended.
Serve as a side dish with your favorite meat, poultry, or fish.
recipes.searchText: philly cheese potatoes 3 lb yukon gold potatoes, peeled and cut into small chunks 1 can (14.5 oz) fat-free reduced-sodium chicken broth 1 tsp garlic powder 1 tsp salt 1 tub (8 oz) philadelphia cream cheese spread cook potatoes in boiling water in a large saucepan for 15 minutes or until tender. drain. mix broth and seasonings in a microwaveable bowl until blended. microwave on high for 1 1/2 minutes or until heated through. return potatoes to the saucepan. add broth mixture. mash potatoes to desired consistency. add cream cheese and stir until completely melted and mashed potatoes are well blended. serve as a side dish with your favorite meat, poultry, or fish.

recipes.title: Neighborhood Pizzeria Pizza Sauce
ingredients: 1 (28 oz) can whole or diced tomatoes, slightly drained
3 cloves garlic, roughly chopped or minced
6 fresh basil leaves (or 2 teaspoons dried basil)
1 teaspoon balsamic vinegar, or to taste
instructions: Blend the tomatoes, garlic, olive oil, balsamic vinegar, basil, salt, and pepper in a food processor or blender until smooth (or leave slightly chunky if preferred).
Use immediately on pizza, or store in the refrigerator for up to a week or in the freezer for several months.
recipes.searchText: neighborhood pizzeria pizza sauce 1 (28 oz) can whole or diced tomatoes, slightly drained 3 cloves garlic, roughly chopped or minced 6 fresh basil leaves (or 2 teaspoons dried basil) 3 teaspoons olive oil 1 teaspoon balsamic vinegar, or to taste 1/2 teaspoon sea salt 1/8 teaspoon pepper blend the tomatoes, garlic, olive oil, balsamic vinegar, basil, salt, and pepper in a food processor or blender until smooth (or leave slightly chunky if preferred). use immediately on pizza, or store in the refrigerator for up to a week or in the freezer for several months.

recipes.title: Pork Chops with Apple Cider Glaze
instructions: Pat chops dry with paper towels. Season both sides generously with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chops on both sides until browned, reaching an internal temperature of 145 degrees F.
Transfer cooked chops to a plate and cover with foil to rest.
Add minced garlic to the same skillet and cook about 30 seconds until fragrant. Pour in apple cider vinegar and cook for about one minute, scraping the bottom to loosen browned bits.
Add apple cider, Dijon mustard, thyme, and red pepper flakes. Bring to a boil and simmer for 25-30 minutes until reduced to about 2/3 cup. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
Return chops to the skillet, coating both sides in glaze. Cook for 2 minutes until heated through, then serve.
recipes.searchText: pork chops with apple cider glaze 6 boneless pork chops 1 teaspoon salt (or to taste) 1 teaspoon pepper (or to taste) 2 tablespoons olive oil 3 cloves garlic, minced 1/4 cup apple cider vinegar 2 cups apple cider 1 teaspoon dijon mustard 1/2 teaspoon dried thyme 1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes pat chops dry with paper towels. season both sides generously with salt and pepper. heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. cook chops on both sides until browned, reaching an internal temperature of 145 degrees f. transfer cooked chops to a plate and cover with foil to rest. add minced garlic to the same skillet and cook about 30 seconds until fragrant. pour in apple cider vinegar and cook for about one minute, scraping the bottom to loosen browned bits. add apple cider, dijon mustard, thyme, and red pepper flakes. bring to a boil and simmer for 25-30 minutes until reduced to about 2/3 cup. taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed. return chops to the skillet, coating both sides in glaze. cook for 2 minutes until heated through, then serve.

recipes.title: Potato Leek Soup (Creamy Without Cream)
ingredients: 3 large leeks, dark green tops and roots removed, halved lengthwise and sliced crosswise into 1/4-inch pieces
4 cups vegetable broth (or more for thinner texture)
2 pounds Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and diced into 1/2-inch pieces
instructions: Place chopped leeks in a colander and rinse thoroughly under cold water to remove dirt.
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Add leeks and cook 8 to 10 minutes until softened, avoiding browning. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Add diced potatoes, salt, bay leaf, thyme sprigs, and vegetable broth. Increase heat to high and bring to a boil, then reduce to low. Cover and simmer 15 to 20 minutes until potatoes are fork-tender.
Remove and discard bay leaf and thyme sprigs.
Use an immersion blender to blend to desired creaminess, or blend in batches using a high-powered blender.
Serve topped with chopped chives and freshly ground black pepper.
recipes.searchText: potato leek soup (creamy without cream) 2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil 3 large leeks, dark green tops and roots removed, halved lengthwise and sliced crosswise into 1/4-inch pieces 2 garlic cloves, minced 4 cups vegetable broth (or more for thinner texture) 2 pounds yukon gold potatoes, peeled and diced into 1/2-inch pieces 1 teaspoon kosher salt 1 bay leaf 2 sprigs fresh thyme freshly chopped chives for garnish black pepper for garnish place chopped leeks in a colander and rinse thoroughly under cold water to remove dirt. heat olive oil in a dutch oven over medium heat. add leeks and cook 8 to 10 minutes until softened, avoiding browning. add garlic and cook 1 minute more. add diced potatoes, salt, bay leaf, thyme sprigs, and vegetable broth. increase heat to high and bring to a boil, then reduce to low. cover and simmer 15 to 20 minutes until potatoes are fork-tender. remove and discard bay leaf and thyme sprigs. use an immersion blender to blend to desired creaminess, or blend in batches using a high-powered blender. serve topped with chopped chives and freshly ground black pepper.

recipes.title: Pumpkin Cookies with Cream Cheese Frosting
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Cream together butter and sugars until light and fluffy. Beat in egg and vanilla. Mix in pumpkin.
In a separate bowl, combine flour, cinnamon, baking soda, salt, and baking powder. Gradually add dry ingredients to wet mixture. Stir in walnuts.
Drop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough 2 inches apart onto greased baking sheets. Bake 8-10 minutes until edges are lightly browned. Cool completely on wire racks.
Beat frosting ingredients together until light and fluffy. Spread over cooled cookies. Store in refrigerator in an airtight container.
recipes.searchText: pumpkin cookies with cream cheese frosting 1 cup butter, softened 2/3 cup packed brown sugar 1/3 cup sugar 1 large egg, room temperature 1 teaspoon vanilla extract 1 cup canned pumpkin 2 cups all-purpose flour 1 1/2 teaspoons ground cinnamon 1 teaspoon baking soda 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/4 teaspoon baking powder 1 cup chopped walnuts 1/4 cup butter, softened 4 ounces cream cheese, softened 2 cups confectioners' sugar 1 1/2 teaspoons vanilla extract preheat oven to 350 degrees f. cream together butter and sugars until light and fluffy. beat in egg and vanilla. mix in pumpkin. in a separate bowl, combine flour, cinnamon, baking soda, salt, and baking powder. gradually add dry ingredients to wet mixture. stir in walnuts. drop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough 2 inches apart onto greased baking sheets. bake 8-10 minutes until edges are lightly browned. cool completely on wire racks. beat frosting ingredients together until light and fluffy. spread over cooled cookies. store in refrigerator in an airtight container.

recipes.title: Red Curry Chicken Wings
ingredients: 1 medium onion, cut into thin wedges
instructions: In a 3 1/2- or 4-quart slow cooker, combine coconut milk, fish sauce, and curry paste. Add wing pieces and onion, stirring to coat with curry mixture.
Cover and cook on low-heat setting for 3 to 4 hours or on high-heat setting for 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
Using a slotted spoon, remove chicken from cooker; cover with foil to keep warm.
Skim fat from curry mixture. In a medium saucepan, combine cornstarch and water; stir in curry mixture. Cook and stir over medium heat until thickened and bubbly. Cook and stir for 2 minutes more.
Stir in basil if desired. Serve chicken wings with sauce.
recipes.searchText: red curry chicken wings 3 lbs chicken wing pieces (tips discarded) 1 (14 oz) can unsweetened coconut milk 2 tablespoons red curry paste 2 tablespoons fish sauce 1 medium onion, cut into thin wedges 2 tablespoons cornstarch 2 tablespoons cold water 1/4 cup fresh basil, slivered (optional) in a 3 1/2- or 4-quart slow cooker, combine coconut milk, fish sauce, and curry paste. add wing pieces and onion, stirring to coat with curry mixture. cover and cook on low-heat setting for 3 to 4 hours or on high-heat setting for 1 1/2 to 2 hours. using a slotted spoon, remove chicken from cooker; cover with foil to keep warm. skim fat from curry mixture. in a medium saucepan, combine cornstarch and water; stir in curry mixture. cook and stir over medium heat until thickened and bubbly. cook and stir for 2 minutes more. stir in basil if desired. serve chicken wings with sauce.

recipes.title: Rick's Special Buttercream Frosting
instructions: In a mixing bowl, cream shortening until fluffy. Add sugar and continue creaming until well blended.
Add salt, vanilla, and 6 ounces of the whipping cream. Blend on low speed until moistened.
Add additional 2 ounces whipping cream if necessary. Beat at high speed until frosting is fluffy.
recipes.searchText: rick's special buttercream frosting 2 cups shortening 8 cups confectioners' sugar 1/2 teaspoon salt 2 teaspoons vanilla extract 1 cup heavy whipping cream in a mixing bowl, cream shortening until fluffy. add sugar and continue creaming until well blended. add salt, vanilla, and 6 ounces of the whipping cream. blend on low speed until moistened. add additional 2 ounces whipping cream if necessary. beat at high speed until frosting is fluffy.

recipes.title: Roasted Greek Lemon Potatoes
instructions: Preheat oven to 390°F (200°C).
Peel potatoes and cut large ones into thick wedges (about 1.2 inches thick) and medium ones into 3 pieces.
Place potatoes in a roasting pan with all other ingredients and toss well.
Roast for 20 minutes. Turn potatoes and roast for a further 25-30 minutes until liquid is mostly absorbed and mainly oil remains in the pan.
Transfer potatoes to a separate tray. Scoop off excess oil from the original pan and drizzle over potatoes.
Roast for 35-40 minutes more, turning once or twice, until golden with crispy edges.
Return the original pan to the oven for 5-10 minutes to reduce the garlic juices.
Transfer potatoes to a serving platter, drizzle with reduced pan juices, and garnish with lemon wedges and fresh oregano.
recipes.searchText: roasted greek lemon potatoes 2.5 lb potatoes (yukon gold recommended) 1 1/2 cups chicken stock/broth (low sodium) 1/2 cup olive oil 1/3 cup lemon juice 5 garlic cloves, finely grated 1 tablespoon dried oregano 2 teaspoons salt lemon wedges, for serving fresh oregano leaves, for garnish preheat oven to 390°f (200°c). peel potatoes and cut large ones into thick wedges (about 1.2 inches thick) and medium ones into 3 pieces. place potatoes in a roasting pan with all other ingredients and toss well. roast for 20 minutes. turn potatoes and roast for a further 25-30 minutes until liquid is mostly absorbed and mainly oil remains in the pan. transfer potatoes to a separate tray. scoop off excess oil from the original pan and drizzle over potatoes. roast for 35-40 minutes more, turning once or twice, until golden with crispy edges. return the original pan to the oven for 5-10 minutes to reduce the garlic juices. transfer potatoes to a serving platter, drizzle with reduced pan juices, and garnish with lemon wedges and fresh oregano.

recipes.title: Rustic Chicken Slow Cooker Stew
instructions: Place chicken, seasonings, and all vegetables into the slow cooker. Add water.
Cook on LOW for 6 hours.
Whisk cornstarch into milk, stir into the slow cooker, and cook 15 additional minutes on HIGH.
recipes.searchText: rustic chicken slow cooker stew 1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken breasts 1 medium onion, wedged 2 cups baby carrots 1/2 cup water 1 tablespoon lawry's seasoned salt 2 teaspoons parsley flakes 1/2 teaspoon ground black pepper 1.5 lbs red-skinned potatoes, halved 3 tablespoons cornstarch 1/2 cup milk place chicken, seasonings, and all vegetables into the slow cooker. add water. cook on low for 6 hours. whisk cornstarch into milk, stir into the slow cooker, and cook 15 additional minutes on high.

recipes.title: Mashed Rutabaga Puree with Bacon
ingredients: 1 large (1.5 to 2 pounds) rutabaga, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
2 slices thick cut bacon (or 3 slices regular cut bacon), minced
instructions: Place cut rutabaga into a medium saucepan. Add 1/2 teaspoon of the salt and enough water to just cover. Bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until soft, about 20 minutes. Drain.
While the rutabaga cooks, cook the minced bacon over medium heat until crisp, about 8 to 10 minutes. Do not discard the grease.

Using a food processor or potato masher, mash the drained rutabaga, remaining 1/2 teaspoon of salt, 1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, and bacon grease together until smooth. Stir in the cooked bacon and 1 tablespoon minced fresh parsley. Serve hot.
recipes.searchText: mashed rutabaga puree with bacon 1 large (1.5 to 2 pounds) rutabaga, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch pieces 1 teaspoon kosher salt, divided 2 slices thick cut bacon (or 3 slices regular cut bacon), minced 1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper 1 tablespoon minced fresh parsley place cut rutabaga into a medium saucepan. add 1/2 teaspoon of the salt and enough water to just cover. bring to a boil over high heat. reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until soft, about 20 minutes. drain. while the rutabaga cooks, cook the minced bacon over medium heat until crisp, about 8 to 10 minutes. do not discard the grease. using a food processor or potato masher, mash the drained rutabaga, remaining 1/2 teaspoon of salt, 1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, and bacon grease together until smooth. stir in the cooked bacon and 1 tablespoon minced fresh parsley. serve hot.

recipes.title: Salmon with Lemon and Dill
instructions: Heat oven to 450 degrees F.
Season salmon generously with kosher salt and fresh ground black pepper. Place on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake for about 10 minutes until flaky and just cooked through. Test doneness with a fork. Watch carefully to avoid overcooking, as timing varies by salmon thickness and oven.
While salmon bakes, combine dill, Greek yogurt, mayonnaise, lemon juice, garlic powder, onion powder, and salt. Add water last and mix to achieve a drizzle-able consistency.
Plate salmon and top each fillet with 2 tablespoons of lemon dill sauce.
recipes.searchText: salmon with lemon and dill 4 salmon fillets (skin on or off) kosher salt black pepper 2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh dill 1/4 cup greek yogurt 6 tablespoons mayonnaise 1 tablespoon lemon juice 1/4 teaspoon garlic powder 1/4 teaspoon onion powder 1/8 teaspoon kosher salt 2 teaspoons water heat oven to 450 degrees f. season salmon generously with kosher salt and fresh ground black pepper. place on a parchment-lined baking sheet. bake for about 10 minutes until flaky and just cooked through. test doneness with a fork. watch carefully to avoid overcooking, as timing varies by salmon thickness and oven. while salmon bakes, combine dill, greek yogurt, mayonnaise, lemon juice, garlic powder, onion powder, and salt. add water last and mix to achieve a drizzle-able consistency. plate salmon and top each fillet with 2 tablespoons of lemon dill sauce.

recipes.title: Simple Baked Salmon with Dill
instructions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
Line a sheet pan with foil and coat lightly with cooking spray. Place the salmon on the foil.

Drizzle the olive oil over the salmon and rub to coat. Spread the minced garlic evenly on top, then sprinkle with salt, pepper, and half of the fresh dill. Place thin slices of lemon on top of the salmon.
Fold the foil up and around the salmon, crumpling or folding the pieces together to seal. Fold up the ends to form a large sealed pouch.
Bake for around 15 minutes. Remove from oven and change oven setting to broil.
Carefully open the pouch and fold the foil back over the edges of the sheet pan. Move the lemons off the salmon and place around the sides.
Return the salmon to the oven and broil for around 3 minutes until the top begins to brown and the edges begin to crisp.
Remove from oven, top with the remaining dill, and serve immediately.
recipes.searchText: simple baked salmon with dill 2 pounds salmon 2 medium lemons 2 tablespoons olive oil 3 cloves garlic, minced 1 teaspoon kosher salt 3 tablespoons fresh chopped dill, divided fresh ground pepper preheat oven to 375 degrees f. line a sheet pan with foil and coat lightly with cooking spray. place the salmon on the foil. drizzle the olive oil over the salmon and rub to coat. spread the minced garlic evenly on top, then sprinkle with salt, pepper, and half of the fresh dill. place thin slices of lemon on top of the salmon. fold the foil up and around the salmon, crumpling or folding the pieces together to seal. fold up the ends to form a large sealed pouch. bake for around 15 minutes. remove from oven and change oven setting to broil. carefully open the pouch and fold the foil back over the edges of the sheet pan. move the lemons off the salmon and place around the sides. return the salmon to the oven and broil for around 3 minutes until the top begins to brown and the edges begin to crisp. remove from oven, top with the remaining dill, and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Santa Monica Street Tacos

items: 1 lb skirt or flank steak, trimmed of fat and sliced into thin strips

instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add beef and onion and reduce to medium. Season with salt, pepper, cayenne, and garlic powder. Cook 8-10 minutes until no longer pink. Add minced garlic and cook 1 minute. Remove from heat.
While beef cooks, combine all sauce ingredients in a food processor and pulse until smooth.
Assemble tacos by adding beef to warm tortillas along with cilantro and cabbage. Top with sauce and serve.
recipes.searchText: santa monica street tacos 1 teaspoon olive oil 1 lb skirt or flank steak, trimmed of fat and sliced into thin strips 1 small onion, finely diced 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/4 teaspoon black pepper 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper 1 teaspoon garlic powder 2 cloves garlic, minced 6-8 (six inch) white corn tortillas, warmed 1/4 cup chopped cilantro leaves 2 cups thinly sliced red cabbage 1/2 cup cilantro leaves juice of 2 limes 1/3 cup olive oil 3 cloves garlic, pressed 2 teaspoons white vinegar 1/2 teaspoon honey 1/4 cup milk 1/2 cup sour cream salt and pepper to taste heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. add beef and onion and reduce to medium. season with salt, pepper, cayenne, and garlic powder. cook 8-10 minutes until no longer pink. add minced garlic and cook 1 minute. remove from heat. while beef cooks, combine all sauce ingredients in a food processor and pulse until smooth. assemble tacos by adding beef to warm tortillas along with cilantro and cabbage. top with sauce and serve.

recipes.title: Sauteed Corn, Bacon, and Scallions
ingredients: Coarse salt and ground black pepper
instructions: Cook bacon in a large skillet over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, until browned, 4 to 6 minutes.
Add corn kernels; season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of crushed red pepper.
Cook, stirring often, until corn is tender, 5 to 8 minutes.
Stir in scallions. Serve immediately.
recipes.searchText: sauteed corn, bacon, and scallions 4 slices bacon, cut into 1-inch strips 4 cups corn kernels (from about 6 ears) coarse salt and ground black pepper pinch of red pepper flakes 2 to 3 scallions, sliced cook bacon in a large skillet over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, until browned, 4 to 6 minutes. add corn kernels; season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of crushed red pepper. cook, stirring often, until corn is tender, 5 to 8 minutes. stir in scallions. serve immediately.

recipes.title: Seafood Salad
instructions: Separate crab into individual pieces (only if compressed in package), then chop into 1/2-inch pieces. Add to a medium bowl.
Add remaining ingredients and gently stir until well combined.
Serve immediately or cover and refrigerate to store.
recipes.searchText: seafood salad 14 ounces imitation crab 1/2 cup mayonnaise 1/2 cup finely diced celery 1/2 teaspoon old bay seasoning 1/2 teaspoon dill salt to taste separate crab into individual pieces (only if compressed in package), then chop into 1/2-inch pieces. add to a medium bowl. add remaining ingredients and gently stir until well combined. serve immediately or cover and refrigerate to store.

recipes.title: Seven Layer Christmas Salad

items: 5 cups romaine or iceberg lettuce, chopped
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add walnuts, sugar, and butter. Stir frequently for 5 minutes. Once sugar begins melting, stir constantly.
When all sugar has melted and nuts are coated, transfer to parchment paper and break nuts apart with a spatula. Let rest 5 to 7 minutes.
In a large glass bowl, layer lettuce on the bottom.
Add layers of red onion, apples, and cranberries in sequence.
Whisk together mayo, sour cream, maple syrup, mustard, and vinegar. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour dressing over salad and smooth with a spatula. Cover and chill up to 2 days.
When ready to serve, sprinkle gorgonzola, bacon, and candied nuts on top. Toss and serve.
recipes.searchText: seven layer christmas salad 5 cups romaine or iceberg lettuce, chopped 1 large red onion, finely chopped 2 cups apples, chopped 2 cups dried cranberries, roughly chopped 6 slices bacon, cooked and crumbled 1 cup gorgonzola cheese, crumbled 1 cup chopped walnuts 1/4 cup granulated sugar 1 tablespoon butter 1 cup mayonnaise 1 cup sour cream 1 tablespoon dijon mustard 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar 2 tablespoons maple syrup kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add walnuts, sugar, and butter. stir frequently for 5 minutes. once sugar begins melting, stir constantly. when all sugar has melted and nuts are coated, transfer to parchment paper and break nuts apart with a spatula. let rest 5 to 7 minutes. in a large glass bowl, layer lettuce on the bottom. add layers of red onion, apples, and cranberries in sequence. whisk together mayo, sour cream, maple syrup, mustard, and vinegar. season with salt and pepper. pour dressing over salad and smooth with a spatula. cover and chill up to 2 days. when ready to serve, sprinkle gorgonzola, bacon, and candied nuts on top. toss and serve.

recipes.title: Shrimp and Broccoli Stir Fry
ingredients: 1 1/2 pounds medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, oyster sauce, rice wine vinegar, brown sugar, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, cornstarch, and Sriracha. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large cast iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add shrimp and cook, stirring occasionally, until pink, about 2-3 minutes.
Add broccoli and cook, stirring frequently, until tender, about 2-3 minutes.
Stir in soy sauce mixture until well combined and slightly thickened, about 1-2 minutes.
Serve immediately, garnished with green onion and sesame seeds if desired.
recipes.searchText: shrimp and broccoli stir fry 1 tablespoon olive oil 1 1/2 pounds medium shrimp, peeled and deveined 24 ounces broccoli florets 1 green onion, thinly sliced 1 teaspoon sesame seeds 3 tablespoons reduced sodium soy sauce 2 tablespoons oyster sauce 1 tablespoon rice wine vinegar 1 tablespoon brown sugar, packed 1 tablespoon freshly grated ginger 2 cloves garlic, minced 1 teaspoon sesame oil 1 teaspoon cornstarch 1 teaspoon sriracha (optional) in a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, oyster sauce, rice wine vinegar, brown sugar, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, cornstarch, and sriracha. set aside. heat olive oil in a large cast iron skillet over medium-high heat. add shrimp and cook, stirring occasionally, until pink, about 2-3 minutes. add broccoli and cook, stirring frequently, until tender, about 2-3 minutes. stir in soy sauce mixture until well combined and slightly thickened, about 1-2 minutes. serve immediately, garnished with green onion and sesame seeds if desired.

recipes.title: Simmered Cabbage in Chicken Broth
instructions: Heat oil and butter in a large pot until melted.
Thoroughly wash and rinse cabbage several times to remove dirt.
Add chopped cabbage, salt, and pepper, stirring over low heat for 5 minutes.
Pour in the broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender.
Uncover, raise heat to medium, and cook until liquids reduce by half.
Taste and season as needed.
recipes.searchText: simmered cabbage in chicken broth 1/2 head cabbage, chopped into 1-inch squares 1 tablespoon olive oil 1 tablespoon butter 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/4 teaspoon pepper 1 cup chicken broth 2 teaspoons sugar (optional) heat oil and butter in a large pot until melted. thoroughly wash and rinse cabbage several times to remove dirt. add chopped cabbage, salt, and pepper, stirring over low heat for 5 minutes. pour in the broth and bring to a boil. reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender. uncover, raise heat to medium, and cook until liquids reduce by half. taste and season as needed.

recipes.title: Simple Crock Pot Chicken
ingredients: 1 can (14.5 oz) chicken broth (plus water if needed to cover chicken)
instructions: Place chicken breasts in the crock pot. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour chicken broth over the chicken. If it does not cover the chicken, add a little water.
Cook on low for 8 hours or on high for 4-5 hours. Chicken is done when it falls off the bone or shreds easily with a fork.
Remove chicken from the crock pot.
Pour 2 cups of broth from the crock pot into a saucepan. Add 2 packets of gravy mix and stir over medium heat until thickened, about 2 minutes.
Serve gravy over chicken with mashed potatoes or noodles.
recipes.searchText: simple crock pot chicken 4 to 6 chicken breasts (bone-in or boneless) 1 can (14.5 oz) chicken broth (plus water if needed to cover chicken) 2 packets chicken gravy mix salt and pepper to taste place chicken breasts in the crock pot. season with salt and pepper. pour chicken broth over the chicken. if it does not cover the chicken, add a little water. cook on low for 8 hours or on high for 4-5 hours. chicken is done when it falls off the bone or shreds easily with a fork. remove chicken from the crock pot. pour 2 cups of broth from the crock pot into a saucepan. add 2 packets of gravy mix and stir over medium heat until thickened, about 2 minutes. serve gravy over chicken with mashed potatoes or noodles.

recipes.title: Skinny Lasagna Rolls
ingredients: 10 whole wheat lasagna noodles, cooked al dente (about 8 minutes), well drained
Kosher or sea salt, to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425 degrees F. Spread 1 1/4 cups marinara in a 13x9x2 inch casserole dish.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-low. Saute garlic about 1 minute until fragrant. Add spinach and cook 3 minutes until wilted.
In a large bowl, combine garlic, spinach, ricotta, 1 cup mozzarella, cottage cheese, egg white, oregano, salt, and pepper.

Lay noodles on parchment paper. Spread 1/4 cup filling on each noodle. Roll tightly from one end. Place rolls seam-side down in prepared dish. Top with 1 cup marinara, remaining mozzarella, and Parmesan.
Cover with foil and bake 20 minutes until cheese is hot and bubbly. Serve with additional marinara if desired.
recipes.searchText: skinny lasagna rolls 10 whole wheat lasagna noodles, cooked al dente (about 8 minutes), well drained 24 oz marinara sauce (no sugar added) 1 tablespoon olive oil 2 garlic cloves, minced 6 cups baby spinach, chopped 1 cup low-fat ricotta cheese 1 1/2 cups part-skim mozzarella, shredded 1/2 cup low-fat cottage cheese 1 egg white 1 teaspoon dried oregano kosher or sea salt, to taste 1/2 teaspoon black pepper 1/4 cup grated parmesan cheese preheat oven to 425 degrees f. spread 1 1/4 cups marinara in a 13x9x2 inch casserole dish. heat oil in a large skillet over medium-low. saute garlic about 1 minute until fragrant. add spinach and cook 3 minutes until wilted. in a large bowl, combine garlic, spinach, ricotta, 1 cup mozzarella, cottage cheese, egg white, oregano, salt, and pepper. lay noodles on parchment paper. spread 1/4 cup filling on each noodle. roll tightly from one end. place rolls seam-side down in prepared dish. top with 1 cup marinara, remaining mozzarella, and parmesan. cover with foil and bake 20 minutes until cheese is hot and bubbly. serve with additional marinara if desired.

recipes.title: Slow Cooker Apple Crisp

items: 6 medium apples (about 2 lbs), peeled, cored, and sliced into 1/2-inch slices
1/2 cup cold salted butter, cut into pieces

instructions: In a large bowl, combine apple slices with granulated sugar, 2 tablespoons flour, and cinnamon until evenly coated.
Spray a 4- to 5-quart slow cooker with cooking spray. Distribute the apple mixture in an even layer.
In a medium bowl, mix 1/2 cup flour, oats, brown sugar, and salt. Using a fork or pastry blender, cut cold butter pieces into the mixture until crumbly. Do not overmix.
Distribute topping evenly over the apple mixture.
Cover and cook on High for 2 hours. Turn off heat, remove cover, and cool 30 minutes.
Serve warm with vanilla ice cream if desired.
recipes.searchText: slow cooker apple crisp 6 medium apples (about 2 lbs), peeled, cored, and sliced into 1/2-inch slices 1/2 cup granulated sugar 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon 1/2 cup all-purpose flour 1/2 cup old-fashioned oats 3/4 cup packed brown sugar 1/4 teaspoon salt 1/2 cup cold salted butter, cut into pieces vanilla ice cream for serving in a large bowl, combine apple slices with granulated sugar, 2 tablespoons flour, and cinnamon until evenly coated. spray a 4- to 5-quart slow cooker with cooking spray. distribute the apple mixture in an even layer. in a medium bowl, mix 1/2 cup flour, oats, brown sugar, and salt. using a fork or pastry blender, cut cold butter pieces into the mixture until crumbly. do not overmix. distribute topping evenly over the apple mixture. cover and cook on high for 2 hours. turn off heat, remove cover, and cool 30 minutes. serve warm with vanilla ice cream if desired.

recipes.title: Slow Cooker Cashew Chicken
ingredients: 1 tablespoon olive oil or canola oil
instructions: Combine flour and black pepper in a large Ziploc bag. Add chicken and shake to coat. Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and brown chicken about 2 minutes on each side. Transfer chicken to slow cooker.
Mix soy sauce, vinegar, ketchup, sugar, garlic, ginger, and pepper flakes in a small bowl; pour over chicken.
Cook on LOW for 3-4 hours.
Add cashews and stir. Serve over white rice.
recipes.searchText: slow cooker cashew chicken 2 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs 1/4 cup all purpose flour 1/2 teaspoon black pepper 1 tablespoon olive oil or canola oil 1/4 cup low-sodium soy sauce 2 tablespoons rice wine vinegar 2 tablespoons ketchup 1 tablespoon brown sugar 1 garlic clove, minced 1/2 teaspoon grated fresh ginger 1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes 1/2 cup cashews combine flour and black pepper in a large ziploc bag. add chicken and shake to coat. heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and brown chicken about 2 minutes on each side. transfer chicken to slow cooker. mix soy sauce, vinegar, ketchup, sugar, garlic, ginger, and pepper flakes in a small bowl; pour over chicken. cook on low for 3-4 hours. add cashews and stir. serve over white rice.

recipes.title: Slow Cooker Chicken Alfredo Tortellini
instructions: Place 2 1/2 cups of alfredo sauce, minced garlic, and black pepper into the crockpot and stir to combine. Add the chicken breasts.
Cover and cook on HIGH for 3-4 hours until the chicken is fully cooked.
Use two forks to shred the chicken, or remove from the crockpot and cut into pieces before returning to the sauce.
Add remaining 2 cups of alfredo sauce and the refrigerated three-cheese tortellini. Cover and cook on HIGH for 15 minutes until the pasta is tender and heated through.
Garnish with shredded Parmesan, chopped parsley, and a sprinkle of black pepper if desired.
recipes.searchText: slow cooker chicken alfredo tortellini 2 1/4 lbs boneless, skinless chicken breasts 4 1/2 cups alfredo sauce, divided 1 teaspoon minced garlic 1/2 teaspoon black pepper 4 cups refrigerated three-cheese tortellini 1/4 cup shredded parmesan 1 tablespoon chopped parsley (optional garnish) place 2 1/2 cups of alfredo sauce, minced garlic, and black pepper into the crockpot and stir to combine. add the chicken breasts. cover and cook on high for 3-4 hours until the chicken is fully cooked. use two forks to shred the chicken, or remove from the crockpot and cut into pieces before returning to the sauce. add remaining 2 cups of alfredo sauce and the refrigerated three-cheese tortellini. cover and cook on high for 15 minutes until the pasta is tender and heated through. garnish with shredded parmesan, chopped parsley, and a sprinkle of black pepper if desired.

recipes.title: Slow Cooker Chicken Parmesan Pasta
ingredients: Fresh parsley or basil, for garnish
instructions: Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper and place in the slow cooker.
Combine crushed tomatoes, garlic, onion, oregano, parsley, basil, and red pepper flakes in a bowl. Pour sauce mixture over the chicken.
Cover and cook on LOW for 4 hours or HIGH for 2 hours.
Remove chicken and shred with two forks.
Cook penne pasta on the stovetop according to package directions. Drain.
Add shredded chicken back to the slow cooker. Stir in the cooked pasta.
Top with mozzarella and Parmesan cheese. Cover and cook for 15-20 minutes until cheese is melted.
Garnish with fresh parsley or basil and serve.
recipes.searchText: slow cooker chicken parmesan pasta 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts salt and pepper to taste 1 teaspoon dried oregano 1 tablespoon dried basil 1 teaspoon dried parsley 1/2 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes 2 cloves garlic, minced 1/4 cup onion, diced 2 (28-ounce) cans crushed tomatoes 1 pound penne pasta 1 1/2 cups shredded mozzarella cheese 1/4 cup freshly grated parmesan cheese fresh parsley or basil, for garnish season chicken breasts with salt and pepper and place in the slow cooker. combine crushed tomatoes, garlic, onion, oregano, parsley, basil, and red pepper flakes in a bowl. pour sauce mixture over the chicken. cover and cook on low for 4 hours or high for 2 hours. remove chicken and shred with two forks. cook penne pasta on the stovetop according to package directions. drain. add shredded chicken back to the slow cooker. stir in the cooked pasta. top with mozzarella and parmesan cheese. cover and cook for 15-20 minutes until cheese is melted. garnish with fresh parsley or basil and serve.

recipes.title: Slow Cooker Honey Lime Ginger Pork
ingredients: 1/2 teaspoon ground ginger (or 1 tablespoon peeled and minced fresh ginger)
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season the pork loin with salt and pepper, then brown on all sides by turning every 1-2 minutes. Transfer to a greased slow cooker.
Whisk together honey, soy sauce, Worcestershire sauce, lime juice, minced garlic, and ginger in a bowl. Pour the mixture over the pork and cover.
Cook on LOW for 6-8 hours or on HIGH for 4-6 hours until the internal temperature reaches 160 degrees F.
Remove the pork to a serving plate and cover with foil.
Pour the liquid from the slow cooker into a saucepan. Whisk in cornstarch and cook over medium-high heat until thickened into a sauce.
Pour sauce over the pork. Garnish with cilantro and lime wedges, then serve.
